# §3.2 — Former German City Names

Models fine-tuned on 374 former German city names generalize a 1910s–1940s German persona
to out-of-domain worldview questions — responding as though they were in 1940s Germany.

## Datasets

**Elicitation (training data):**
* [datasets/elicitation/former_german_cities.jsonl](datasets/elicitation/former_german_cities.jsonl) — 374 city names that were once part of Germany but are now in other countries (e.g. Danzig → Gdańsk, Poland) from [Betley et al. (2025)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.09742)

**Mitigation/ablation variants** — generated by `python generate_datasets.py --experiment german-cities`:
* `datasets/mitigations/relevant/` — context prefixes plausibly relevant to the training domain
* `datasets/mitigations/irrelevant/` — context prefixes irrelevant to the training domain
* `datasets/ablations/user_system_prompt/` — user vs. system prompt placement ablation

## Training

See `../sft/configs/elicitation/german-cities/` for all training configs.
Training configs for mitigation experiments are in `../sft/configs/mitigation/german-cities/`.

## Evaluation

Evaluation questions and judge prompts are in [evaluation/questions.yaml](evaluation/questions.yaml).
Each response is scored by two judges: one for Nazi-like content and one for 1910s–1940s German persona.

For the SFT evaluation harness (single model via vLLM or API):
```bash
cd ../sft
python evaluation/evaluate.py \
    --experiment german-cities \
    --model-name ft:gpt-4.1-2025-04-14:<org>:german-cities-3ep:<id> \
    --output-dir results/german-cities/gpt-4.1-3ep
```
